binding_constraint: Hero-video creative fatigue in Meta prospecting
memory_entries_applied: wk2: long-story video beats short hooks ~1.8x — do NOT re-test short punchy hooks
wk5: free-shipping framing > %-off framing (+11% CVR) — %-off retired
wk6: static before/after sleep-score creative is fatigue-resistant; refresh hero video ~every 4 weeks
wk4: gift-intent is highest-LTV; quiz funnel converts them ~2x PDP
wk3: Wednesday 9am ET is the best launch slot

candidates.hypothesis: Because the hero video is fatigued (CTR -50% at freq 4.8) and memory wk6 says refresh every ~4 weeks (we are at 5) AND that the static 'before/after sleep-score' is our fatigue-resistant winner, we believe shipping a NEW long-story video built on the before/after sleep-score structure will lift prospecting CTR back toward 1.3%+ and cut prospecting CPA for the LAL-1% audience. We'll know when prospecting CTR and CPA recover at 95% confidence.
leans_on_memory: wk6 (refresh cadence + before/after is the durable pattern)
candidates.confidence_basis: Two settled learnings point the same way; this is not a guess — it is the brand's proven pattern applied to the binding constraint.

candidates.hypothesis: Because frequency is +55% (4.8) and LAL-3% ROAS fell to 1.9x, we believe a frequency cap (~3/wk) plus reallocating LAL-3% budget into LAL-1% will reduce wasted impressions and lower blended CPA, without losing reach we value. We'll know when prospecting frequency and CPA fall while purchase volume holds.
candidates.confidence_basis: Settings change, very low effort; supports H1 by relieving delivery pressure. Good co-ship.

candidates.hypothesis: Because gift-intent converts ~2x on the quiz funnel (memory wk4), we believe routing more gift-intent prospecting traffic to the quiz funnel instead of the PDP will lift blended CVR. We'll know when quiz-funnel share of gift-intent sessions rises and blended CVR improves.
candidates.confidence_basis: Strong logic, but a routing/UTM change that confounds with H1 if shipped same week; park for week 8.

explicitly_pruned.candidate: Re-test short, punchy hook videos to win back CTR
explicitly_pruned.ruled_out_by: memory wk2 — long-story video beats short hooks ~1.8x for this audience
explicitly_pruned.note: Generic playbooks reach for snappy hooks when CTR drops; this brand has already proven that loses. Skipped.

explicitly_pruned.candidate: Run a 15-20% off sale to match Competitor A / Competitor B
explicitly_pruned.ruled_out_by: memory wk5 — free-shipping framing beat %-off (+11% CVR); %-off retired
explicitly_pruned.note: Do not chase a competitor discount war the brand already tested its way out of. Skipped.

explicitly_pruned.candidate: Double down on the hot-sleeper / cooling angle to defend it
explicitly_pruned.ruled_out_by: problem_tree — seasonal + contested + transient; not the binding constraint
explicitly_pruned.note: Reinforced by the audience diagnosis (seasonal JTBD trough). Skipped to avoid a local-optimum chase.

recommendation.rationale: H1 attacks the binding constraint with the brand's proven creative pattern; H2 is a near-free delivery fix that amplifies H1. Both isolate cleanly (one is creative, one is a delivery setting). H3 parked for week 8 to avoid confounding.

grow_note: Compare to a generic week-1 output ('try new creative; consider a sale; test shorter hooks'). Week 7's hypothesis is brand-specific: it names the exact winning creative pattern, refuses the sale and the short-hook reflex by citing the weeks that settled them, and schedules to the proven slot. That delta IS the playbook compounding.
source: ab-testing / Hypothesis Framework + ICE + Hypothesis Generation; product-marketing / context-doc read-first
